time_coverage: articles published roughly 2019-2024, discussing data through those years
units: n/a (narrative articles with embedded charts/figures, not raw data tables)
license_terms: Stanford Iranian Studies Program publication; no explicit reuse license stated on site; standard web copyright presumed
notes: Both target entry points were directly reachable: https://purl.stanford.edu/yd955cs5721 (HTTP 200) is the Stanford Digital Repository collection landing page for the Iran 2040 archive, but it only links out to a SearchWorks catalog search for the collection's member items (https://searchworks.stanford.edu/catalog?f[collection][]=yd955cs5721) -- that SearchWorks endpoint is blocked by the SAME F5/TSPD JavaScript bot-challenge confirmed against cbi.ir in the prior session (verified via identical signature: window["bobcmn"], /TSPD/ tokens in response body), so the collection's individual archived files/data could not be enumerated or downloaded in this pass. The publications page (https://iranian-studies.stanford.edu/iran-2040-project/publications) WAS directly reachable and listed ~18 publication titles, each linking to its own article page under /publications/<slug> or /iran-2040-project/publications/<slug>. IMPORTANT DEVIATION FROM TASK ASSUMPTION: the site has been redesigned as a Drupal-based web-native publishing platform -- checked 12 publication landing pages (7 under /publications/, 5 under /iran-2040-project/publications/) and NONE exposed a standalone downloadable PDF file (no .pdf hrefs, no print/alternate link tags, no iframes/embeds); each publication is instead a full HTML article with inline text, embedded chart images (as responsive <picture>/webp figures), and captions -- there is no separate 'PDF report with data appendix' to fetch. Given that, downloaded the 5 most economically substantive publication articles as complete HTML pages (the actual content unit the site now serves) rather than fabricating or skipping the task: financing gap/BoP, central banking, oil economics, sanctions-era macroeconomy, and non-oil exports under sanctions. These are real, verified HTML documents (correct <title> tags, substantial body text, embedded data figures) -- not bot-challenge pages.

failures.reason: F5/TSPD JS bot-challenge (same signature as cbi.ir), blocks curl -- could not enumerate/download individual Stanford Digital Repository collection items for yd955cs5721
